ens: noun. An entity or being; an existing thing, as opposed to a quality or attribute.
inn: noun. Any establishment where travellers can procure lodging, food, and drink.
inset: verb. To set in; infix or implant.
intense: adjective. Of a characteristic: extreme or very high or strong in degree; severe; also, excessive, towering.
nee: interjection. no, used to express no as a quantity, i.e. not any, like German kein /Dutch geen /French rien.
nene: noun. The Hawaiian goose, Branta sandvicensis, which was designated the state bird of Hawaii in 1957.
nest: noun. A structure built by a bird as a place to incubate eggs and rear young.
net: noun. A mesh of string, cord or rope.
nett: adjective. Alternative spelling of net (remaining after expenses or deductions).
nine: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 9.
nit: noun. The egg of a louse.
see: verb. To perceive or detect someone or something with the eyes, or as if by sight.
seine: noun. A long net having floats attached at the top and sinkers (weights) at the bottom, used in shallow water for catching fish.
sennet: noun. a signal call given on a cornet or trumpet for entrance or exit on a theatrical stage
sennit: noun. braided cord or fabric of such small stuff as plaited rope yarns
sent: verb. simple past and past participle of send
sente: noun. A subdivision of currency, equal to one hundredth of a Lesotho loti.
sentient: adjective. Experiencing sensation, thought, or feeling.
set: verb. To put (something) down, to rest.
sett: noun. The system of tunnels that is the home of a badger.
sin: noun. A violation of divine will or religious law.
sine: noun. In a right triangle, the ratio of the length of the side opposite an angle to the length of the hypotenuse.
sit: verb. To be in a position in which the upper body is upright and supported by the buttocks.
site: noun. The place where anything is fixed; situation; local position
sitten: verb. past participle of sit; alternative form of sat.
snit: noun. A temper; a bad mood.
stein: noun. A beer mug, usually made of ceramic or glass.
stet: noun. A symbol used by proofreaders and typesetters to indicate that a word or phrase that was crossed out or changed should remain as it
stint: verb. To stop (an action); cease, desist.
tee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter T / t.
teen: noun. Synonym of teenager: a person between 13 and 19 years old (inclusive).
ten: noun. NATO, ICAO, ITU & IMO radiotelephony code for 10, used only with o'clock to indicate direction.
tenet: noun. An opinion, belief, or principle that is held as absolute truth by someone or especially an organization.
tennis: noun. A sport played by two players (or four in doubles), who alternately strike the ball over a net using racquets.
tense: noun. The property of indicating the point in time at which an action or state of being occurs or exists.
tent: noun. A pavilion or portable lodge consisting of skins, canvas, or some strong cloth, stretched and sustained by poles, used for sheltering
tet: noun. Alternative form of teth (“Semitic letter”).
tin: noun. A malleable, ductile, metallic element, resistant to corrosion, with atomic number 50 and symbol Sn.
tine: noun. A spike or point on an implement or tool, especially a prong of a fork or a tooth of a comb.
tit: noun. A person's breast or nipple.
esne: noun. A hireling of servile status; slave.
innest: adjective. superlative form of in: most in: most popular, most fashionable
insee: verb. To see into; to observe acutely.
isn: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Isanzu.
ist: noun. A practitioner or supporter of an ism.
neet: noun. Alternative letter-case form of NEET.
nete: noun. In Ancient Greek musical theory, the highest-pitched fixed note in the farther tetrachord on a lyre, always pitched a perfect fourth
neti: noun. Nasal irrigation performed using a neti pot as part of Hatha yoga.
nis: noun. A nix; a hobgoblin, especially one that resides in a farm house.
nne: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ngandyera.
sentine: noun. A place for dregs and dirt; a sink; a sewer.
setnet: noun. Alternative form of set net.
sten: noun. Alternative letter-case form of Sten
stent: noun. A slender tube inserted into a blood vessel, a ureter or the oesophagus in order to provide support and to prevent disease-induced
stie: noun. Obsolete form of stye (“eye infection”).
tnt: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tontemboan.
tsine: noun. A Malayan wild ox, Bos javanicus.
